title: aeonium canariense subsp. latifolium
intro: Aeonium canariense subsp. latifolium, also known as the Canary Island Houseleek, is a succulent plant species that belongs to the Aeonium genus. It is a rare and less common variety of Aeonium canariense, which is native to the Canary Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. Aeonium canariense subsp. latifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems, with potential health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved immune function.
